title: Alkermes — Psychiatry — Synthesis
html:
Alkermes Psychiatry Cross-Study Synthesis

Generated: 2026-04-26 Studies analyzed: 6 Files parsed: Lybalvi Sales Force Tactical Optimization Proposal (Dec 2024), Aristada SOV Tracker Q4'23, Aristada SOV Tracker Q1'24, Aristada SOV Tracker Q2'25, Lybalvi PET 2025 SOW, Vivitrol PET 2025 SOW Findings extracted: Manual extraction from ZoomRx deliverables + web research on market dynamics Method: Cross-study pattern matching against thesis tracker

1. Cross-Study Patterns

Pattern 1: Lybalvi Investment Is Increasing While Aristada Investment Is Contracting — Alkermes Is Rotating the Portfolio

This is the clearest signal across all ZoomRx engagements. Lybalvi gets a $338,701 multichannel PET (2 full + 2 partial waves, 180 unique HCPs) plus a proposed $89,500 Sales Force Tactical Optimization. Aristada gets a quarterly 3-minute SOV tracker with 125 HCPs. Vivitrol gets a single-wave $117,435 PET.

Lybalvi PET SOW: Full omnichannel tracking including personal and non-personal promotion, aided message recall, visual aid usage, sample dispense rates, patient request/grant rates. Competitors tracked: Vraylar, Caplyta, Rexulti — AND Cobenfy. This is a premium engagement that tracks every dimension of promotional effectiveness.

Aristada SOV Tracker: Stripped-down 3-minute survey measuring only reach/frequency/SOV. No message recall, no call quality deep dive, no tactical recommendations. And the call plan contracted from 27.4K to 9.3K NPIs between Q1'24 and Q2'25.

What this means together: Alkermes is explicitly investing in Lybalvi growth and managing Aristada for efficiency. The research budget allocation mirrors the corporate strategy: Lybalvi is the growth engine, Aristada is a cash harvester. ZoomRx should align its account strategy accordingly — Lybalvi upsells are the priority.

Pattern 2: Abilify Is Pulling Away in LAIs, and Nobody (Including Alkermes) Is Fighting Back

Across three ARISTADA SOV tracker waves (Q4'23, Q1'24, Q2'25), the pattern is unmistakable:

  • Q4'23: Abilify captured highest SOV, ARISTADA declined. "ABILIFY captured the highest SOV in Q4'23; ARISTADA SOV decreased, driven by the UZEDY launch."
  • Q1'24: ARISTADA SOV "increased slightly, reaching parity with INVEGA." Abilify remained market leader.
  • Q2'25: Abilify's increased frequency drove its SOV to 40%. ARISTADA, INVEGA, and Teva each converged around ~20%.

The trend line is clear: Abilify went from leader to dominant leader. ARISTADA didn't decline — it just didn't grow while everyone else consolidated around it. In Segment A (high-decile HCPs), ARISTADA's reach and frequency actually declined in Q2'25 while SOV held — meaning Alkermes reps are calling fewer top prescribers but those prescribers are hearing more ARISTADA mentions per call. This is a concentration strategy, not a growth strategy.

What this means together: The LAI market is bifurcating into Abilify-dominant and everyone-else. Alkermes appears to have accepted this and is managing ARISTADA as a profitable niche rather than competing for LAI leadership. ZoomRx's ARISTADA SOV tracker confirms this narrative quarter after quarter.

Pattern 3: Alkermes Is Proactively Tracking Cobenfy — They See the Threat

The 2025 Lybalvi PET SOW explicitly includes: "COBENFY reach, frequency, share of voice tracked along with sample-related questions." This is not routine competitive tracking — Cobenfy was approved only 2 months before the SOW was signed (Sept 2024 approval, Nov 2024 SOW). Adding a brand-new competitor to a tracking study this quickly signals urgency.

The Tactical Optimization proposal (Dec 2024) is also telling. It asks: "Which competitors messages hurt LYBALVI the most?" and proposes analyzing "Impact of one Key Competitor messages on LYBALVI prescribing for SZ and BPD Patients." The unnamed "key competitor" is almost certainly Cobenfy — the only new entrant that could hurt Lybalvi's growth trajectory.

What this means together: Alkermes sees Cobenfy as a strategic threat and is investing in intelligence to understand and counter it. This is the most fertile ground for ZoomRx upsell: Cobenfy-specific intelligence that goes beyond SoV tracking into message vulnerability analysis, prescriber segment switching patterns, and tactical countermeasures.

Pattern 4: Vivitrol Is Being Studied for Competitive Defense, Not Growth Innovation

The Vivitrol PET SOW ($117,435, single wave) is explicitly focused on competitive tracking: "Assess the quality of rep interactions for VIVITROL, SUBLOCADE and BRIXADI." The research objectives are defensive: understand how Vivitrol reps compare to Sublocade/Brixadi reps, identify message gaps, and track unaided awareness.

This is not a growth-oriented research design. There are no exploratory modules (patient journey, unmet need assessment, segmentation). The study tracks AUD generic competition ("Understand top-of-mind message recall of generic AUD products") — suggesting Vivitrol faces downward pressure even in its AUD stronghold.

What this means together: Alkermes is managing Vivitrol defensively ahead of potential 2027 generic entry. The PET is a health check, not a growth engine. ZoomRx's opportunity here is modest unless Vinoth can reframe the study around AUD market expansion intelligence.

2. Thesis Check

Thesis 1: Cobenfy's ARISE failure gives Lybalvi a 2-year window

Status: STRENGTHENED The SOW signing timeline confirms Alkermes was already preparing defensive intelligence before ARISE failed. Post-ARISE, the window thesis is even stronger — Cobenfy is limited to monotherapy, its analyst estimates are cut by 40-50%, and Lybalvi's 4-year weight data is the strongest real-world evidence in the class. No contradicting evidence in ZoomRx data. Action: Track Cobenfy SoV vs Lybalvi in the Q1'25 full PET wave. If Cobenfy SoV is <10% among Lybalvi's target prescribers, the window thesis holds.

Thesis 2: Aristada SOV stability is deceptive

Status: CONFIRMED Three waves of SOV data (Q4'23, Q1'24, Q2'25) show ARISTADA flat at ~20% while Abilify grew from ~30% to ~40%. Call plan contracted 66%. Revenue guidance flat for 2026. Every data point confirms managed decline. Action: Position the next ARISTADA SOV readout as a portfolio optimization conversation, not a competitive intelligence one. Help Alkermes make data-driven decisions about where to redeploy ARISTADA resources.

Thesis 3: Vivitrol's AUD growth masking OUD share loss

Status: NEEDS DATA The 2025 Vivitrol PET is the first study that could validate or refute this thesis. No PET results yet — the wave fields in 2025 with final report due June 2025. Alkermes management's comment about "growth in the alcohol dependence indication" supports the thesis directionally. Action: When Vivitrol PET results arrive, segment findings by indication. If AUD message recall and prescribing intent are stronger than OUD equivalents, the thesis strengthens.

Thesis 4: ZoomRx's biggest opportunity is the Cobenfy defensive intelligence play

Status: STRENGTHENED The explicit Cobenfy tracking in the Lybalvi SOW, combined with the tactical optimization proposal's competitor messaging analysis, confirms Alkermes is actively seeking this intelligence. The $89,500 tactical optimization is the entry point. Action: Monitor Sara Farina's communications about the tactical optimization proposal status. If accepted, use the findings to demonstrate value and propose Cobenfy-specific follow-on work.

3. ZoomRx Account Intelligence

Revenue Summary (Active + Proposed)

Engagement Status Value Brand
Lybalvi Multichannel PET 2025 Active $338,701 Lybalvi
Vivitrol PET 2025 Active $117,435 Vivitrol
ARISTADA SOV Tracker Active (ongoing) Est. $40-60K/yr Aristada
Sales Force Tactical Optimization Proposed $89,500 Lybalvi
Total Active ~$500-520K
Total incl. Proposed ~$590-610K

Key Contacts

Name Title Context
John Fritch VP, Data, Analytics & Insights Signed Lybalvi PET SOW. Decision maker.
Kurt Stauble Sr. Dir., MR & CI Signed Vivitrol PET SOW. CI-focused.
Sandra Maria Rodrigues (IQVIA TPA contact) Data access coordination.
Sara Farina Sr. Manager, ZoomRx Account lead. Signed both SOWs for ZoomRx.

Upsell Opportunities (Ranked)

  1. Lybalvi Sales Force Tactical Optimization ($89,500, proposed) — highest probability, already scoped
  2. Cobenfy Impact Deep-Dive (new proposal, $80-120K est.) — leverage Cobenfy tracking data from Lybalvi PET to build a dedicated Cobenfy countermeasure study
  3. Lybalvi BPD-specific PET (new concept, $120-150K est.) — current PET tracks SZ+BPD combined; a BPD-specific wave could capture the Vraylar/Caplyta battleground separately
  4. Cross-brand promotional optimization (new concept, $100-150K est.) — model the Lybalvi/Aristada resource allocation question analytically
